message.content:
i(base) benkaron@Mac python_models % git checkout master
error: Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by checkout:
        views/standard_data_refresh/08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql
Please commit your changes or stash them before you switch branches.
Aborting
(base) benkaron@Mac python_models %

I'm trying to save these changes while I temporarily checkout another branch, what can I do?

content.text:
You can use `git stash` to temporarily save your changes. Here are your options:

**Quick solution:**
```bash
git stash
git checkout master
# ... do work on master ...
git checkout eng-6486-follow-up-requests-for-proj-reduce-cx-contacts-in-onboarding
git stash pop
```

**Or if you want to be more descriptive:**
```bash
git stash save "WIP: intercom conversations changes"
git checkout master
```

Then later when you return:
```bash
git checkout eng-6486-follow-up-requests-for-proj-reduce-cx-contacts-in-onboarding
git stash pop  # or git stash apply if you want to keep it in stash
```

The stash will save both your modified and untracked files (the `??` files) if you use `git stash -u` to include untracked files as well.
